Information: Dedicated as Cort Memorial Bridge

Displayed Sign Text: Barbara Fritchie Bridge
Information: Barbara Fritchie, a Unionist and central figure in Frederick’s history, was immortalized in the eponymous poem titled “Barbara Frietchie” by John Greenleaf Whittier (using the German spelling of her name.)

Displayed Sign Text: C. Philip Nichols Memorial Bridge
Information: Dedicated to C. Philip Nichols, Sr. who served two terms in the MD House of Delegates from 1954-1962 and was instrumental in this bridge’s construction.

Displayed Sign Text: This Highway Dedicated to John S. Arnick
Information: Dedicated to John S. Arnick, Baltimore native and US Marine Corps veteran, who served three separate spans in the MD House of Delegates ranging from 1969 through 2006.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated as Henrietta Lacks Way
Information: Dedicated to Henrietta Lacks whose cervical cancer cells were the source of the HeLa cell line, the first immortalized cell line. The HeLa cell line continues to be a source of invaluable medical data to the present day.

Displayed Sign Text: In Memory of Nathan A. Blizzard
Information: Dedicated to Nathan A. Blizzard who perished in an automobile accident at age 20 on his way to work on a dairy farm on December 5, 1997.

Displayed Sign Text: This Travel Information Center is named in honor of Governor Harry R. Hughes...
Information: Named in honor of Harry Roe Hughes, 57th Governor of Maryland for two terms, 1979-1987 who served as the state's first Secretary of Transportation from 1971-1979.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator James Clark, Jr.
Information: Dedicated to James Clark, Jr., Ellicott City native and former president of the MD State Senate from 1979-1983.

Displayed Sign Text: In Memory of Ofc. Duke G. Aaron III Maryland Transportation Authority Police
Information: Dedicated to Maryland Transportation Authority Police Officer Duke G. Aaron III, who was killed in the line of duty when his patrol car was struck from behind on July 20, 2004.

Displayed Sign Text: Father John Stanislaus Cuddy Memorial Bridge
Information: Named in honor of Father John Stanislaus Cuddy, a popular local priest at nearby St. Martins, who was killed in 1928 when a train struck his automobile. The incident spurred the bridge construction for safer crossing.

Displayed Sign Text: Clara Barton Parkway
Information: Named in honor of Clara Barton, founder of the American Red Cross, who spent her final 15 years at her home in nearby Glen Echo which doubled as an early headquarters of the organization.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to James G. Barrett
Information: Dedicated to James G. Barrett, former Worcester County Commissioner, who advocated for the dualization of US-113.

Displayed Sign Text: This Parkway Dedicated In Memory of John P. Tawes
Information: Dedicated to John P. Tawes, who advocated for the construction of a dual carriageway through the main section of Crisfield.

Displayed Sign Text: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Ida G. Ruben
Information: Dedicated to Ida G. Ruben who represented District 20 in both the MD House of Delegates and MD Senate from 1975 to 2007 and advocated for the expansion of the FDA campus in White Oak.

Displayed Sign Text: This Bridge Dedicated to Senator Leo E. Green
Information: Dedicated to Leo E. Green, who represented Prince George's County in the MD Senate from 1983 to 2007.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Police Officer III Noah A Leotta | Don't Drink And Drive
Information: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Officer Noah A. Leotta who was killed in the line of duty while pulling over a suspected drunk driver on December 3, 2015. As a result, Senate Bill 945 was passed, colloquially referred to as “Noah’s Law."

Displayed Sign Text: George Washington Highway
Information: Named in honor of George Washington, the 1st President of the United States.

Displayed Sign Text: C. Philip Nichols Memorial Bridge
Information: Dedicated to C. Philip Nichols, Sr. who served two terms in the MD House of Delegates from 1954-1962 and was instrumental in this bridge’s construction.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Cpl. Scott A. Wheeler Howard County Police Dept
Information: Dedicated to Howard County Police Corporal Scott A. Wheeler who was killed in the line of duty while initiating a traffic stop on June 18, 2007.

Displayed Sign Text: Judge Lloyd L ""Hot Dog"" Simpkins Rest Area
Information: Named in honor of Judge Lloyd L. “Hot Dog” Simpkins, lifelong Somerset County resident, who retired in 1990 at the age of 70 but continued to preside over cases on a part-time basis until 2010, making him the oldest sitting judge in Maryland history.

Displayed Sign Text: The U.S. Route 13 Travel Information Center is Dedicated to the Memory of Mark O. Pilchard
Information: Dedicated to Mark O. Pilchard who served the citizens of the Lower Eastern Shore as a member of the MD House of Delegates from 1959-1966 and 1979-1989 and was instrumental in the creation of the rest area.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator Philip C. Jimeno
Information: Dedicated to Philip C. Jimeno who represented District 31 for 28 years, serving terms in both the MD House of Delegates and Senate from 1979 to 2007.

Displayed Sign Text: Harriet Elizabeth Brown Memorial Parkway
Information: Named in honor of Harriett Elizabeth Brown, Calvert County school teacher who fought for equal pay regardless of race and became the catalyst for the Maryland Teachers Pay Equalization Law, the first equalization law in Maryland.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Delegate Tyras Bunk Athey
Information: Dedicated to Tyras Snowden “Bunk” Athey, Burtonsville native, former MD Secretary of the State who served in the MD House of Delegates from 1967 to 1993.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Dr Charles I Ecker
Information: Dedicated to Charles Isaac Ecker, Maryland native, a career public school administrator and County Executive of Howard County from 1990 to 1998.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Paul Kerman
Information: Dedicated to Paul Kerman who developed Drum Point Beach and served on the Maryland state judicial selection commission.

Displayed Sign Text: In Memory of a Fallen Hero Sgt. Wallace J. Mowbray Maryland State Trooper in the line of duty August 10, 1975
Information: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper Sergeant Wallace Johnson Mowbray who was killed in the line of duty during a traffic stop on August 10, 1975.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to Capt. J.E. Daly Jr. | Cpl J.M. Frontczak
Information: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Captain James Edward Daly, Jr. and Corporal John Michael Frontczak who were killed in the line of duty attempting to arrest a robbery suspect on March 28, 1976.

Displayed Sign Text: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Charles H. Smelser
Information: Dedicated to Charles H. “Bus” Smelser, Uniontown native who represented Carroll and Frederick counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1955 to 1963 and the MD Senate from 1967 to 1994.

Displayed Sign Text: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Charles H. Smelser
Information: Dedicated to Charles H. “Bus” Smelser, Uniontown native who represented Carroll and Frederick counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1955 to 1963 and the MD Senate from 1967 to 1994.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Charles W Elgin Sr
Information: Dedicated to Charles W. Elgin Sr., Poolesville native, who was a community leader and third generation Poolesville resident, served as the Town of Poolesville Commissioner from 1980 to 1994 and considered by many the “father” of Poolesville.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Peace Officer Christopher Nicholson
Information: Dedicated to Smithsburg Police Officer Christopher Shane Nicholson who was killed in the line of duty on December 19, 2007.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to OFC. William D. ""Bill"" Talbert  Montgomery County Police Department
Information: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Officer William D. “Bill” Talbert who was severely injured in the line of duty when struck by a drunk driver and ultimately perished from related complications several years later.

Displayed Sign Text: In Memory of Trooper John E. Sawa Trooper Larry E. Small Maryland State Police
Information: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class John Edward Sawa and Trooper Larry Eugene Small who were killed in the duty when a tractor trailer driven by a drunk driver struck their parked patrol car on March 10, 1987.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator Leonard H. Teitelbaum Maryland State Senate
Information: Dedicated to Leonard H. Teitelbaum who represented District 19 in both the MD House of Delegates and MD Senate from 1987 to 2007.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Governor Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr.
Information: Dedicated to Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr., 60th Governor of Maryland, who prioritized construction of the Intercounty Connector (MD-200) during his administration. This all-electronic toll road opened in 2011 and planning efforts date back to the 1950’s.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Governor Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr.
Information: Dedicated to Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr., 60th Governor of Maryland, who prioritized construction of the Intercounty Connector (MD-200) during his administration. This all-electronic toll road opened in 2011 and planning efforts date back to the 1950’s.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Capt. Joseph A. Mattingly Montgomery County Police Department
Information: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Captain Joseph Aloysius Mattingly who was killed in the line of duty when his patrol car struck a tree on September 13, 2003.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Firefighter Christopher A. Staley Cobb Island Volunteer Fire Dept
Information: Dedicated to Cobb Island Volunteer Firefighter Christopher Allen Staley who died in the line of duty on August 6, 2011 attempting to save a distressed swimmer who also passed.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Capt. Joseph Kroboth, Jr. Halfway Volunteer Fire Co
Information: Dedicated to Volunteer Fire Company of Halfway Captain Joseph Kroboth, Jr. who was killed in the line of duty on May 3, 1998 while directing traffic at the scene of a motor vehicle collision.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Delegate David G. Boschert
Information: Dedicated to David G. Boschert, who served the citizens of Anne Arundel County both as a County Councilman and in the MD House of Delegates and advocated for the expansion of MD-32.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator Laurence Levitan Maryland State Senate
Information: Dedicated to Laurence Levitan who represented District 15 in both the MD House of and MD Senate for over two decades. Senator Levitan was also the Chair of the Budget and Taxation Committee from 1979 to 1994.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Del. K. Bennett Bozman Maryland House of Delegates
Information: Dedicated to K. Bennett Bozman who advocated for the dualization of US-113 and represented Wicomico and Worcester Counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1991 until his death in 2006.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to TFC Anthony Jones Maryland State Police
Information: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class Anthony Jones who was killed in the line of duty when he was struck by a vehicle operated by an intoxicated driver on May 9, 2004.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Sgt. Anthony M. Walker Prince George's County Police Dept
Information: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Sergeant Anthony “Tony” Michael Walker, President of Prince George’s County Fraternal Order of Police, who succumbed to injuries sustained while on patrol in an automobile accident on November 15, 2003.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Corporal Thomas P. Jensen Prince George's County Police Dept.
Information: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Corporal Thomas “Tommy” Paul Jensen, who succumbed to injuries sustained in an automobile accident while responding to a burglary in progress on March 9, 2010.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Sergeant Richard S. Findley Prince George's County Police Dept.
Information: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Sergeant Richard Scott Findley, who was killed in the line of duty when he was intentionally struck by the driver of a stolen automobile on June 27, 2008.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Thomas M. Yeager
Information: Dedicated to Thomas Merle Yeager, who was a member of the Howard County Council from 1975 to 1983 and represented newly formed District 13 in the MD Senate from 1983 to 1994.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated As Alfred B. Hilton Memorial Bridge
Information: Dedicated to Alfred B. Hilton, Havre de Grace native, who was one of 16 African-American Union Army Soldiers who received the Medal of Honor for their service during the Civil War and the only service member from Harford County.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Delegate Norman H Conway
Information: Dedicated to Norman H. Conway, Salisbury native and former President of Salisbury City Council (1982-1987), who represented District 38B in the MD House of Delegates from 1987 to 2015.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to SSGT Charles I. Cartwright Untied States Marine Corps
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Staff Sergeant Charles I. Cartwright who was killed in action in Farah province, Afghanistan on November 7, 2009 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Key Highway
Information: Named in honor of Francis Scott Key, Maryland native and author of our national anthem, "The Star-Spangled Banner."

Displayed Sign Text: Broening Highway
Information: Named in honor of William Frederick Broening, former Mayor of Baltimore for two terms, 1919-1923 and 1927-1931.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to J. Frank Raley, Jr. Founder of Modern St. Mary's County
Information: Dedicated to J. Frank Raley, Jr. who represented St. Mary’s County in the both the MD House of Delegates (1955-1959) and MD Senate (1963-1966) but never stopped advocating for the area. Among other things, Raley is credited with helping establish St. Mary’s College of Maryland as a four-year liberal arts college and was a driving force in the construction of the Thomas Johnson bridge.

Displayed Sign Text: Watermen's Memorial Bridge
Information: Partial inscription from the neighboring Maryland Watermen’s Monument: Dedicated to all watermen in the state of Maryland for their tremendous contribution to our seafood industry, culture and heritage. We pay homage to their unique way of life and may their legacy forever be remembered.

Displayed Sign Text: Battle of North Point War of 1812
Information: The Battle of North Point, fought on September 12, 1814, was a pivotal moment in the War of 1812. One of several commemorations in the immediate area.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to Senator Gwendolyn Greene Britt
Information: Dedicated to Gwendolyn Britt nee Greene, Washington D.C. native and civil rights activist who represented District 47 in the MD Senate from 2003 until her death in 2008 and fought for funding for the construction of CSX bridge over Annapolis Road.

Displayed Sign Text: In Memory Of Our Fallen Heroes TFC William P. Mills Jr. TFC Mark P. Groner Maryland State Troopers
Information: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class William P. Mills, Jr. and Trooper First Class Mark P. Groner both of whom were killed in the line of duty on June 8, 1979 and October 1, 1992, respectively.

Displayed Sign Text: SPC Erik W Hayes US Army Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-29-04 | Scenic Monocacy River
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Specialist Erik W. Hayes, Harney and Thurmont native, who was killed in action in Al Miqdadiyah, Iraq on November 29, 2004 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Stephanie Roper Highway
Information: Dedicated to Stephanie Roper, whose 1982 murder was the catalyst for the creation of a number of victim’s assistance services. It prompted her parents,  Roberta and Vincent Roper, to form the Maryland Crime Victims’ Resource Center, formerly known as the Stephanie Roper Committee and Foundation.

Displayed Sign Text: Stephanie Roper Highway
Information: Dedicated to Stephanie Roper, whose 1982 murder was the catalyst for the creation of a number of victim’s assistance services. It prompted her parents,  Roberta and Vincent Roper, to form the Maryland Crime Victims’ Resource Center, formerly known as the Stephanie Roper Committee and Foundation.

Displayed Sign Text: This Interchange Dedicated to Bishop James R. Peebles Sr.
Information: Dedicated to Bishop James R. Peebles, Sr., who formed nearby Jericho City of Praise with his wife in 1969.

Displayed Sign Text: This road dedicated in the memory of David A. Bramble
Information: Dedicated to David A. Bramble, Fairlee native, who founded the construction company David A. Bramble, Inc., one of the largest employers in the upper Eastern Shore area.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to DFC James Yvorra
Information: Dedicated to Prince George's Fire Department Deputy Fire Chief James G. Yvorra who was killed in the line of duty when he was struck by an automobile while on the scene of an accident on January 28, 1988.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to CPL Nicholas Lee Ziolkowski United States Marine Corps
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal Nicholas Lee Ziolkowski, Towson native, who was killed in action on November 14, 2004 by enemy fire in Fallujah, Iraq while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Maryland Natural Resources Police
Information: Dedicated to the Maryland Natural Resources Police, Maryland’s oldest state police force and one of the nation’s oldest conservation law enforcement agencies, which celebrated 150 years of service in 2018.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator Norman Stone
Information: Dedicated to Senator Norman R. Stone, Jr., Baltimore native, who represented District 6 in the in the Maryland State Senate for 12 terms from 1967 to 2015. Senator Stone is the longest serving senator in Maryland history.

Displayed Sign Text: SSGT William J Kundrat USMC (Raider) Killed 07-10-17
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Staff Sergeant William Joseph Kundrat, Frederick native, who was killed in action during a military training accident in Mississippi on July 10, 2017.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to Speaker of the House R Clayton Mitchell Jr
Information: Dedicated to R. Clayton "Clay" Mitchell Jr., Chestertown native, who represented District 36 in the MD House of Delegates from 1971 to 1992 and was Speaker of the House from 1987 to 1993.

Displayed Sign Text: CPL Kirk J Bosselmann USMC Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-27-04
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal Kirk J. Bosselmann, Dickerson native, who was killed in action in Anbar Province, Iraq on November 27, 2004 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to SGT John F Plunkard US Army
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Sergeant John Francis Plunkard, Frederick native and Frederick High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Thua Thien province of Vietnam on April 8, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Donnie L Drewer
Information: Dedicated to Donnie L. Drewer, Crisfield native, who gave 55 years of dedicated service to the MDOT State Highway Administration and directly influenced nearly every major roadway project in Dorchester, Somerset, Wicomico and Worcester counties from 1988 to 2016.

Displayed Sign Text: CPL Christopher J Coffland US Army Afghanistan Campaign Killed In Action 11-13-2009
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Corporal Christopher James Coffland, Baltimore native, who was killed in action in Afghanistan on November 13, 2009 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: SGT David James Smith USMC Afghanistan Campaign Killed In Action 1-26-10
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Sergeant David James Smith, Frederick native, who was killed in action in the Helmand province of Afghanistan on January 26, 2010 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom. This was the first bridge in Maryland dedicated to a fallen military member.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Delegate Theodore J Sophocleus
Information: Dedicated to Theodore J. Sophocleus, prominent area political official and longtime public servant who represented District 32 in the Maryland House of Delegates from 1999 to 2018.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to LCPL Matthew A Snyder
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Lance Corporal Matthew A. Snyder, Finksburg native and Westminster High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Anbar Province, Iraq on March 3, 2006 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: CPT John Ryan Dennison US Army Ranger -  Iraq Killed In Action 11-15-06
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Captain John Ryan Dennison, Urbana High School alumnus, who was killed in action in Balad, Iraq on November 15, 2006 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Champions of Voter Reform
Information: Dedicated to the ten men of the North Dorchester Democratic Club who in 1986 successfully fought to change the statewide county council election process from at-large voting to a district voting system. The nearby Historical Freedom Shrine commemorates the amendment to the Maryland State Constitution and declares North Dorchester County as the “Birthplace of Voter Reform.”

Displayed Sign Text: SGT Sherwood Reynolds US Army Vietnam War Killed in Action 2-26-68
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Sergeant Sherwood Reynolds, Prince Frederick native, who was killed in action on February 26, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War. Sergeant Reynolds was the first service member from Calvert County to perish in combat in Vietnam.

Displayed Sign Text: A2C Gary Wayne Cosgrave USAF Vietnam War Killed in Action 5-12-67
Information: Dedicated to United States Air Force Airman Second Class Gary Wayne Cosgrave, Frederick native, who was killed in action on May 12, 1967 while supporting the Vietnam War.

Displayed Sign Text: PFC C Franklin Brandenburg US Army - Vietnam War Killed In Action 08-05-67
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class Sergeant Charles Franklin Brandenburg, Frederick native and Frederick High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Quang Ngai Province of South Vietnam on August 5, 1967 while supporting the Vietnam War.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Officer Oliver Smith, Jr
Information: Dedicated to Metropolitan Police Officer Oliver Wendell Smith, Jr. who was killed in the line of duty during a robbery while off duty on February 26, 1997.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to LT (SEAL) Brendan Looney United States Navy
Information: Dedicated to United States Navy Lieutenant (SEAL) John Brendan Aloysius Looney, United States Naval Academy alumnus and Owings Mills native, who was killed in action in Ayatalah Village, Afghanistan on September 21, 2010 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator James Ed DeGrange, Sr.
Information: Dedicated to Senator James “Ed” DeGrange, Sr., prominent area political official who represented District 32 in the Maryland State Senate from 1999 to 2018.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to PFC Russell Milberry United State Army
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class Russell E. Milberry, Union Bridge native, who was killed in action in the Quang Tri province of Vietnam on January 31, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War.

Displayed Sign Text: PFC James Moore Jr Paratrooper Vietnam Killed April 2, 1966
Information: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class James Moore, Jr., Baltimore native, who was killed in action on April 2, 1966 while supporting the Vietnam War.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to Capt Michael S Lawlor United States Marine Corps
Information: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Captain Michael Seamus Lawlor, Timonium native and Towson University alum, who was killed in action along with 3 others marines during a military training accident in Camp Pendleton, California on January 22, 2004.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to PS1 Xavier A Martin United States Navy
Information: Dedicated to United States Navy Petty Officer First Class Xavier A. Martin, Halethorpe native, who perished along with 6 others on the U.S.S. Fitzgerald on June 17, 2017.

Displayed Sign Text: Joppa Road Bridge
Information: Opened July 3, 1958 by Theodore R. McKeldin - Governor - State Roads Commission - Contractor: Williams Construction Co., Inc.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to CPT Robert W Smith US Army - WWII
Information: Dedicated to CPT Robert W Smith US Army, Harford County native, WWII veteran who landed at Normandy on D-Day, who died at the age of 97.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to SGT Eric M Houck SSG Christopher O Moudry
Information: Dedicated to US Army SGT Eric M. Houck, 25, of Baltimore who died from gunshot wounds in the Peka Valley of the Nangarhar Province in Afghanistan & US Army SSG Christopher O. Moudry, 31, of Baltimore; assigned to 7th Squadron, 10th Cavalry Regiment, 1st Brigade, 4th Infantry Division, Fort Hood, Texas; killed Oct. 4 after being attacked by enemy forces using small-arms fire and other weapons in Taji, Iraq.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Brianna N Freeman
Information: In a car crash that took the life of 10 month old Brianna Nicole Freeman, Brianna gave the gift of life to 3 others; her liver to a 2 month old, her kidneys to a 57 year old & her heart to a 3 year old.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Chief Daniel Lister Queen Anne - Hillsboro VFC
Information: The assistant chief of the Queen Anne-Hillsboro Volunteer Fire Company died while working on a scene of a head-on crash with several people entrapped in the wreckage (Aug 2018).

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Sheriff Joseph Lee Somerville St. Mary's County
Information: In 1977, Joseph Lee Somerville became both the first black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in the state of Maryland when he was appointed by the governor to complete the previous sheriff’s term. Somerville went on to win the 1978 election for St. Mary’s County Sheriff, which then made him the first elected Black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in Maryland as well.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Sheriff Joseph Lee Somerville St. Mary's County
Information: In 1977, Joseph Lee Somerville became both the first black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in the state of Maryland when he was appointed by the governor to complete the previous sheriff’s term. Somerville went on to win the 1978 election for St. Mary’s County Sheriff, which then made him the first elected Black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in Maryland as well.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Earle S ""Jock"" Freedman
Information: Earle "Jock" Freedman is a retired bridge engineer and the State of Maryland's "longest-serving" employee.[1][2] Freedman, affectionately known as "Jock", began his career at age 20 in 1950 following graduation from Johns Hopkins University.[3] For over 65 years, Freedman designed and maintained over 2,500 bridges for the Maryland State Highway Administration.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to SPC Toccara Green United States Army
Information: SPC Toccara Green died in Al Asad, Iraq, when multiple improvised explosive devices detonated near her unit during convoy operations. She was assigned to the 57th Transportation Company, 548th Corps Support Battalion, Fort Drum, New York. Died on August 14, 2005.

Displayed Sign Text: Designated as Piscataway Highway
Designated as Piscataway Highway
Dedicated to Hon Henry B ""Hank"" Heller
Information: Henry B. “Hank” Heller, was a Democrat who represented Montgomery County in the House of Delegates for two dozen years. Heller, a longtime educator & union activist who served as president of the Montgomery County Education Association, was elected to represent District 19, which takes in portions of Silver Spring and unincorporated Rockville, in 1986.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator George C Edwards
Information: Senator George Edwards was elected as the State Senator for Maryland District 1 in 2006, which covers Garrett County and parts of Washington and Allegany counties. He defeated Thomas Conlon, both of whom ran for the seat vacated by John J. Hafer who retired after 16 years in the State Senate. Edwards was first elected to the Maryland House of Delegates in 1982 and served for 24 years before running and winning the State Senate seat.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to SGT Mujahid Ramzziddin
Information: Sergeant Mujahid Ramzziddin was shot and killed at approximately 10:15 am while attempting to protect a female subject who was being attacked in a domestic violence incident. Sergeant Ramzziddin, who was off duty and lived nearby, attempted to protect the female and confronted the man who was armed with a shotgun.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to Veterans of Cecil County
Fallen Heroes Bridge Dedicated to Mayor Gerald Wesley Donovan
Information: Gerald Donovan served the citizens of the Town of Chesapeake Beach as the Mayor for 24 years (from 1984-2008), served as the President of the North Beach Volunteer Fire Department and has operated the Rod-N-Reel resort, which continues to serve as the largest employer in the Town.  Mr. Donovan also founded, and hosted, the successful Cancer Gala/ Celebration of Life raising over $5M for the American Cancer Society.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Senator Ed Reilly
Information: The nine-mile portion of Maryland Route 450 from Crofton to Annapolis known as Defense Highway will be officially renamed after Reilly on October 25, 2023. Reilly worked on solutions to flooding along the road in 2018 & 2019.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Deputy Chief Sander B Cohen Office of the State Fire Marshall
Information: Deputy Chief Sander B. Cohen was killed on December 8, 2017 at approximately 10:00 p.m. after stopping to assist FBI Supervisory Special Agent Carlos Wolff who had just been involved in single vehicle accident in the southbound lane of I-270 in the Rockville area.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to the Honorable Karen Montgomery
Information: Karen Slater Montgomery (born August 23, 1935) is an American politician and artist from the state of Maryland. She served as the state senator for the Maryland State Senate from Maryland's 14th District, which includes parts of Silver Spring, Burtonsville, Olney, Barnesville, Brookeville, Laytonsville and Damascus in Montgomery County.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Commissioner Clyde W Unglesbee
Information: Mr. Unglesbee was a Town of Brookeville commissioner, charter president and life member of Olney Chamber of Commerce, member of the board of directors of: Montgomery General Hospital; Sandy Spring National Bank; Asbury Methodist Village; and Sandy Spring Museum, serving two years as chairman.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to PFC Michael A Carter Jr United States Marine Corps
Information: An 18-year-old Marine, who was back near his Maryland hometown recruiting for the Corps, died in a single-vehicle crash.  PFC. Michael Anthony Carter Jr. died Feb. 7 when his Toyota Camry hit a guardrail and went into a ditch, according to Delmarva Now.

Displayed Sign Text: Michael R Powers Libertytown VFD 6-25-2019
Information: Mr. Michael R. Powers, 70, of Libertytown, passed away on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, in the line of duty while operating at an emergency incident scene.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to Detective Jacai Colson Prince George's Co. Police Dept.
Information: Corporal Jacai Colson was shot and killed while responding to an active shooter at the Prince George's County Police Department's District III police station at 7600 Barlowe Road on March 13, 2016.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to PFC Donald H Selby Korean War MIA-POW
Information: On the night of Feb. 11, 1951 Company K of the U.S. Army’s 3rd Battalion, 9th Regiment, 2nd Infantry Division was operating a task force alongside the Republic of Korea’s 16th Infantry Regiment to push back Chinese Communists during the Korean War. The incursion was brief, but deadly, according to the Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency, and Private First Class Donald Harold Selby of Oriole was among those captured by the Chinese. After hostilities ended, repatriated POWs reported Pfc Selby died.

Displayed Sign Text: Dedicated to PFC Thurman B Ellis Army Rangers 1st Batt
Information: Thurman B Ellis from Jarrettsville, MD served with distinction in WWII. A Private First Class in the US Army he was inducted into the service in February 1943 & received his basic training at Camp McCain, MS. He was assigned to the US Army Rangers Company C 1st Ranger Battalion which was part of a composite force of the 6615th Ranger Force (Provisional) with the 1st, 3rd, and 4th Ranger Battalions, 83rd Chemical Mortar Battalion, & the 509th Parachute Infantry Battalion.

Displayed Sign Text: Bridge Dedicated to PFC Charles W Harbert Killed in Action 5-12-1968
Information: PFC Charles Harbert died from gunshot wounds received when hit by hostile small arms fire while on combat operations. He was a member of Company H, 2nd Bn., 502 Infantry, 101st Airborne Division.
